name: NOTAM expiry: rows past valid_until disappear within auto-refresh tick (acceptance bullet D)
method: Probe information_schema for any expiry column (valid_until / expires_on / expires_at / expiry_date) on public.notams. Insert one row with a posted_on of 2024-01-01 (long past) and one current row. Run the dashboard's NOTAM list query and count visible rows — if expiry filtering existed and were applied, count would be 1, not 2.
residual: notams table has no expiry column (valid_until / expires_on / expires_at / expiry_date). Acceptance bullet D for NOTAM expiry cannot be satisfied by the current schema. Either: (a) add a `valid_until` column + auto-refresh filter, or (b) formally drop this acceptance bullet from the spec. Filed as a defect candidate in this report.
acceptance_status: FAIL — required behaviour cannot be observed because the underlying column does not exist; there is no client-side expiry filtering either (verified by lack of any matching grep in the dashboard source).
